intended_use: Fetal EchoScan is a machine learning-based computer-assisted diagnosis (CADx) software device indicated as an adjunct to fetal heart ultrasound examination in pregnant women aged 18 or older undergoin
intended_use_full: Fetal EchoScan is a machine learning-based computer-assisted diagnosis (CADx) software device indicated as an adjunct to fetal heart ultrasound examination in pregnant women aged 18 or older undergoing second-trimester anatomic ultrasound exams. When utilized by an interpreting physician, Fetal EchoScan provides information regarding the presence of any of the following suspicious radiographic findings: overriding artery, septal defect at the cardiac crux, abnormal relationship of the outflow tracts, enlarged cardiothoracic ratio, right ventricular to left ventricular size discrepancy, tricuspid valve to mitral valve annular size discrepancy, pulmonary valve to aortic valve annular size discrepancy, cardiac axis deviation.
ground_truth: Reference standard derived through truthing process in which three pediatric cardiologists assessed the presence or absence of each of the eight findings, with majority voting used to establish ground truth, conducted independently of the Fetal EchoScan device.
other_metrics: Conclusive output rate: 98.8% (95% CI, 97.8-99.3) of exams. Reader study showed mean sensitivity of 0.935 (aided) vs 0.782 (unaided) and mean specificity of 0.970 (aided) vs 0.759 (unaided) for detection of any claimed suspicious finding. Per-finding sensitivities and specificities reported in Table 1 ranged from 0.869 to 0.995 and 0.989 to 1.000 respectively.
